title: Lesson 23
paragraph: At the community center, staff apologize when a room is late and publish updates online. People can rent a small hall for parties. Teachers allow students to attend clubs, close windows after class, copy the schedule, cross the hall safely, and hold a short meeting. If plans fail, staff explain the reason clearly.

words.meaning: to make a book, article, or information available to people
words.example: The club will publish the newsletter online.

words.meaning: to pay money to use a room, house, or object for a time
words.example: We rent a small apartment near the station.
The rules allow students to use tablets in class.

words.meaning: to go to a class, meeting, or event
words.example: I attend a music lesson every Wednesday.

words.meaning: to shut something or stop being open
words.example: Please close the window before you leave.

words.meaning: to make something the same as another thing
words.example: Do not copy your friend's answer.

words.meaning: to go from one side to the other
words.example: Look both ways before you cross the street.

words.meaning: to not succeed or not pass a test
words.japaneseMeaning: 失敗する、不合格になる
words.example: You may fail the test if you do not study.

words.meaning: to keep something in your hand or organize an event
words.example: Please hold this bag while I open the door.
